title: The black-hole information paradox
statement: Hawking's 1975 calculation shows a black hole emits thermal radiation and slowly evaporates. If the radiation is exactly thermal and the hole disappears completely, an initial pure quantum state evolves into a mixed (thermal) state, violating the unitarity of quantum mechanics and destroying information. Reconciling black-hole evaporation with unitarity is the central problem of quantum gravity's low-energy regime. Recent island/replica-wormhole computations reproduce a unitary Page curve for the radiation entropy, but the explicit mechanism by which information escapes the interior remains open.

originators.name: Stephen Hawking
originators.note: 1975 derivation of black-hole radiation; 1976 argument that evaporation breaks predictability (information loss).

ranking.rationale: Pits unitarity against the semiclassical Hawking calculation; needs quantum gravity. Ranked #2 (just below the singularity) because the 2019-2020 island program is concrete, replicable progress — the most tractable of the deep problems.

key_ideas: Hawking radiation is (to leading semiclassical order) thermal, carrying no information about what formed the hole.
The Page curve: if evaporation is unitary, the radiation's entanglement entropy must rise then fall, peaking at the Page time (~half the entropy radiated).
Islands / quantum extremal surfaces: regions of the interior counted as part of the radiation's entropy, reproducing the Page curve from a gravitational computation.
Replica wormholes: subleading gravitational saddles in the Euclidean path integral that supply the entropy turnover.
Tension with the equivalence principle is sharpened by the firewall argument (problem #06).
Open core: even with a unitary Page curve, the physical mechanism transferring information from interior to radiation (and the fate of the interior) is not settled.

approaches.name: Islands / quantum extremal surfaces
approaches.summary: Compute the radiation entropy including 'island' regions; yields the Page curve in semiclassical gravity.

approaches.name: Replica wormholes
approaches.summary: New saddle points of the gravitational path integral that account for the entropy decrease.

approaches.name: Black-hole complementarity
approaches.summary: Interior and exterior descriptions are complementary, not simultaneously accessible (see #07).

approaches.name: Soft hair
approaches.summary: Information stored in soft (zero-energy) graviton/photon modes on the horizon.

approaches.name: Information loss is real
approaches.summary: Bite the bullet: gravity makes evolution non-unitary at the fundamental level (minority view).

papers.title: Particle creation by black holes
papers.note: Commun. Math. Phys. 43, 199-220 (1975). The QFT-in-curved-spacetime calculation showing black holes emit thermal radiation; origin of the paradox. Canonical classic supplied from knowledge.

papers.title: Breakdown of predictability in gravitational collapse
papers.note: Phys. Rev. D 14, 2460 (1976). Hawking's argument that information is lost and evolution is non-unitary, sharpening the paradox. Canonical classic supplied from knowledge.

papers.title: Black holes and entropy
papers.note: Phys. Rev. D 7, 2333 (1973). Proposed black hole entropy proportional to horizon area, the thermodynamic backbone of the paradox. Canonical classic supplied from knowledge.

papers.title: Information in black hole radiation
papers.note: Phys. Rev. Lett. 71, 3743 (1993). Introduced the 'Page curve' / Page time as the unitarity benchmark for radiation entropy. Canonical classic supplied from knowledge.

papers.title: Black holes: complementarity or firewalls?
papers.note: JHEP 02 (2013) 062. The AMPS firewall argument: smooth horizon, unitarity, and effective field theory cannot all hold. Canonical classic supplied from knowledge.

papers.title: Cool horizons for entangled black holes
papers.note: Fortschr. Phys. (2013), 1607 citations. The ER=EPR conjecture relating entanglement to wormholes, proposed as a firewall resolution.

papers.title: Holographic derivation of entanglement entropy from AdS/CFT (Ryu-Takayanagi)
papers.note: Phys. Rev. Lett. 96, 181602 (2006). The holographic entanglement entropy formula underlying island/QES computations. Canonical classic supplied from knowledge.

papers.title: Quantum extremal surfaces: holographic entanglement entropy beyond the classical regime
papers.note: JHEP 01 (2015) 073. Defined the quantum extremal surface prescription central to the island formula. Canonical classic supplied from knowledge.

papers.title: The large N limit of superconformal field theories and supergravity (AdS/CFT)
papers.note: Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 2, 231 (1998). The holographic duality that makes unitary black hole evolution concrete; backbone of modern resolutions. Canonical classic supplied from knowledge.

papers.title: Microscopic origin of the Bekenstein-Hawking entropy
papers.note: Phys. Lett. B 379, 99 (1996). String-theory microstate counting reproducing black hole entropy, evidence for unitary microphysics. Canonical classic supplied from knowledge.

papers.title: The entropy of bulk quantum fields and the entanglement wedge of an evaporating black hole
papers.note: JHEP (2019), 851 citations. Showed quantum-extremal-surface generalized entropy yields a unitary entanglement wedge for an evaporating black hole.

papers.title: Entanglement wedge reconstruction and the information paradox
papers.note: JHEP (2019), 895 citations. QES phase transition at the Page time reproduces the Page curve and avoids a firewall via interior encoding in the radiation.

papers.title: The Page curve of Hawking radiation from semiclassical geometry
papers.note: JHEP (2019), 693 citations. Introduced the explicit 'island' rule for entropy of systems entangled with gravity.

papers.title: Replica wormholes and the entropy of Hawking radiation
papers.note: JHEP (2019), 756 citations. Replica wormhole saddles in the gravitational path integral derive the island rule in JT gravity.

papers.title: Replica wormholes and the black hole interior
papers.note: JHEP (2022), 235 citations. (arXiv 1911.11977) Justifies the Page curve via replica trick and entanglement-wedge reconstruction with the Petz map.

papers.title: The entropy of Hawking radiation
papers.note: Rev. Mod. Phys. (2021), 99 citations. The standard pedagogical review of the island program and fine-grained entropy of Hawking radiation.

papers.title: Soft Hair on Black Holes
papers.note: Phys. Rev. Lett. (2016), 753 citations. Proposed soft (zero-energy) supertranslation hair as a carrier of black hole information.

papers.title: Supertranslation hair of Schwarzschild black hole: a Wilson line perspective
papers.note: JHEP (2019), 11 citations. QFT derivation that an infalling particle implants soft graviton hair on the horizon.

papers.title: Non-isometric codes for the black hole interior from fundamental and effective dynamics
papers.note: JHEP (2023), 9 citations. Backwards-forwards holographic map generalizing the Akers-Engelhardt-Harlow-Penington-Vardhan non-isometric interior code with dynamics.

papers.title: Fuzzballs and Microstate Geometries: Black-Hole Structure in String Theory
papers.note: Snowmass white paper (2022), 51 citations. Survey of the fuzzball / microstate-geometry program as a string-theory resolution.

papers.title: The fuzzball proposal for black holes: an elementary review
papers.note: Fortschr. Phys. (2005), 1071 citations. Foundational review of the fuzzball picture of the black hole interior.

papers.title: A brief history of Hawking's information paradox
papers.note: Europhys. Lett. (2022), 24 citations. Reviews the paradox and a quantum-hair resolution challenging the factorization assumption.

papers.title: The Black Hole Information Paradox: A Fifty-Year Journey
papers.note: Review (2025), 2 citations. Recent survey connecting the paradox to quantum information and AdS/CFT; useful contemporary entry point.

papers.title: Information radiation in BCFT models of black holes
papers.note: JHEP (2019), 171 citations. Holographic BCFT models of evaporating black holes showing the Page-time entanglement-wedge transition.

papers.title: Information retrieval from Hawking radiation in the non-isometric model of black hole interior: theory and quantum simulation
papers.note: Phys. Rev. D (2023), 3 citations. Combines non-isometric interior codes with Hayden-Preskill decoding and an IBM quantum-processor test.

physicists.name: Stephen Hawking
physicists.contribution: Derived black hole thermal radiation (1975) and articulated the information loss problem (1976); later co-proposed soft hair.

physicists.name: Jacob Bekenstein
physicists.contribution: Proposed black hole entropy proportional to horizon area, the thermodynamic foundation of the paradox.

physicists.name: Don Page
physicists.contribution: Introduced the Page curve and Page time, the quantitative criterion for unitary black hole evaporation.

physicists.name: Leonard Susskind
physicists.contribution: Developed black hole complementarity and the holographic principle, and co-proposed ER=EPR.

physicists.name: Juan Maldacena
physicists.contribution: Discovered AdS/CFT and co-developed the island rule, replica wormholes, and ER=EPR.

physicists.name: Joseph Polchinski
physicists.contribution: Co-author of the AMPS firewall argument that sharpened the modern form of the paradox.

physicists.name: Ahmed Almheiri
physicists.contribution: Co-author of AMPS firewalls and lead author of the island-formula and replica-wormhole papers.

physicists.name: Geoffrey Penington
physicists.contribution: Derived the Page curve from quantum extremal surfaces and co-developed replica wormholes and non-isometric interior codes.

physicists.name: Netta Engelhardt
physicists.contribution: Co-defined the quantum extremal surface prescription central to the island computation of radiation entropy.

physicists.name: Andrew Strominger
physicists.contribution: String-theory microstate entropy counting with Vafa and co-proposer of soft hair on black holes.

simulator_angle: Not directly computable in a classical-GR ray tracer, but the semiclassical input — the Hawking temperature and evaporation timescale (now in the simulator's thermo/hawking.hpp) — is the starting point of the paradox; the simulator can visualize the shrinking horizon over an (illustrative) evaporation timeline.

metadata.coverage_note: Assembled 25 verified, genuinely-relevant papers and 10 physicists. Method: ran 9 Consensus paper-search queries (mcp__plugin_bio-research_consensus__search) across the themes — unitarity/Hawking radiation, Page curve, islands/QES, island formula, soft hair, firewalls/complementarity, entanglement-wedge reconstruction, Hayden-Preskill scrambling, the entropy review, fuzzballs, average-subsystem entropy, and non-isometric interior codes. 15 papers carry real Consensus result URLs in identifier.url (Almheiri et al. entropy-of-bulk-fields, Penington QES, the two replica-wormhole papers, the Page-curve-from-semiclassical-geometry island paper, the RMP review, Maldacena-Susskind ER=EPR, Hawking-Perry-Strominger soft hair, Choi-Akhoury, DeWolfe non-isometric codes, Bena-Martinec-Mathur-Warner and Mathur fuzzball reviews, the 50-year-journey and Calmet brief-history reviews, Rozali et al. BCFT, and Li et al. quantum simulation). The remaining 10 are canonical landmarks supplied from knowledge with bibliographic notes and verifiable arXiv ids / DOIs (Hawking 1975 & 1976, Bekenstein 1973, Page 1993, AMPS 2013, Ryu-Takayanagi 2006, Engelhardt-Wall 2015, Maldacena 1998 AdS/CFT, Strominger-Vafa 1996); no titles, authors, ids, or URLs were invented. Tiers reflect that this is an OPEN problem: most resolution-level claims (islands, replica wormholes, soft hair, fuzzballs, non-isometric codes) are labeled open_conjecture or speculative_extension, while AdS/CFT, RT, and QES machinery are established_theory and Hawking radiation / Bekenstein entropy are analytic_classical. No observational_constraint papers were included because the information paradox currently has no direct observational test. Did not pad; this is the genuine high-relevance set.
